objective: Implement a fork-only runtime/build fix so nightly transformers pinning no longer depends on a git binary, while preserving existing Qwen3.5 FlashInfer strategy and deterministic version behavior.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible fork-local changes to Docker nightly install behavior and runtime documentation across Dockerfile, docs/runtime-compatibility.md, README.md, and aiwf.request.json.
acceptance_criteria: Nightly transformers install path uses immutable GitHub archive tarball for `TRANSFORMERS_REF` instead of `git+https` so build/runtime no longer depends on git for this pinned install.
Deterministic pinning remains unchanged (`VLLM_NIGHTLY_VERSION` and `TRANSFORMERS_REF` exact pins preserved).
Existing Qwen3.5 runtime strategy and FlashInfer guardrails remain unchanged.
Docs are updated so runtime/install wording matches archive-based install behavior.
Validation gate commands complete: py_compile, config_doctor, JSON parse, and docker buildx bake --print.
tests_expected: python -c "import json; json.load(open('.runpod/hub.json')); json.load(open('aiwf.request.json')); print('json ok')"
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback is fork-local git revert of this change set to restore the prior `git+https` transformers install path. If archive URL fetch regressions appear, temporary mitigation is to pin `VLLM_NIGHTLY=false` with stable `VLLM_VERSION=0.16.0` for affected builds while preparing a new pinned `TRANSFORMERS_REF` archive commit. Re-run validation gate before redeploy.
deploy_plan: Deploy fork-only in staged rollout (1->2->4 workers). At each stage verify cold start, successful model load, and one representative inference before scaling.
threat_model: Main risks are (1) larger CUDA devel image increasing build/pull time and (2) upstream FlashInfer wheel/toolchain drift causing runtime variance. Mitigations: keep version pins deterministic, keep runtime fallback guard to FLASH_ATTN on toolchain probe failure, fail fast for missing flashinfer on Qwen3.5, keep rollout staged 1->2->4, and retain fork-local rollback path.
